frameworks.name: Bayesian schema learning with representational splitting
frameworks.one_line: Large prediction errors trigger splitting of representations to acquire new schemas.

frameworks.name: neural network model of schema learning
frameworks.one_line: Connectionist account compared against the Bayesian splitting model.

frameworks.name: single-representation interleaved learning
frameworks.one_line: Non-splitting account predicting interleaving is required to avoid catastrophic interference.

core_question: Does schema learning rely on representational splitting triggered by large prediction errors, allowing blocked curricula to avoid catastrophic interference?
key_assumptions: schemas are discrete representational units that can be split
large prediction errors signal the need for a new representation
curriculum structure (blocked vs interleaved) shapes schema composition
manipulations: timing of blocked training insertion: early vs late in learning
key_findings: blocked curricula produced better performance than interleaved curricula
a Bayesian splitting model captures the blocked-curriculum advantage
inserting blocked training early yields better learning than inserting it later
interpretations: catastrophic interference can be avoided by splitting representations at large prediction errors
prediction errors function to 'carve nature at its joints' across multiple schemas
